violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH CFP at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC place can opener into 3-bay sink to be washed, rinsed and sanitize. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

The doors to the outside, located in the sides of the building, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the inside of a cabinet have an accumulation of old rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
The plumbing under the food prep sink was in poor repair (active leak observed). New Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was under 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200-400 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024

Milk, eggs, and sour cream in the, 'cafe' area were observed held at 47-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings throughout the establishment and a dead mouse observed in dry storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024

Several doors to the outside, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024

The small refrigeration unit in the, 'cafe' area was observed not holding adequate ambient temperature. PIC instructed to take unit out of service until repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024

The condensate from the ice machine is draining into the food prep sink. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into an approved drain system. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
A wall in the mop sink/laundry room was observed in poor repair (heavy water damage causing large gap or opening to form). New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
The ceiling and walls in the food prep area of the, 'cafe' are covered with material that is not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

Wood is used in the construction of the stand for prep sink. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has an accumulation of bio-film. PIC stated that ice machine will be turned off and sanitized on a monthly basis. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2023
